items.t: — June exports hit CHF 2.4B on 1.3M pieces, the year's first double-digit month; France doubles to CHF 250M, the CHF 200–500 band grows 54.1%
— the tape idles on the floor with one quiet week left before the July 28–29 Fed meeting; Monday's Kitco board closed $4,000.30
— the NUM says De Beers' two-year pause puts 1,134 mine jobs and 80 sales staff into Section 189A consultation
— Rio Tinto processes the leftovers of a mine that closed in March; H1 output −51%, sales end with 2026, and a major exits diamonds
— Watches of Switzerland's statutory pre-tax profit jumps 76%, six weeks after rebuffing a reported £1.75B approach; IDEX says buyers are circling again
— H1 jewelry revenue grows on a 19% higher average ticket while unit sales fall, per Tenoris; June alone +13%
— same-store sales surge on gold (+50%) as diamonds fall 17%; mainland China diamond sales −58% while a VAT change pushes buyers to Hong Kong
— the white metal holds Monday's 1.22% gain and keeps leading the complex; platinum 1,573, palladium 1,240
— a natural two-row strand makes £39,680 on a £10,000 estimate at Bonhams as Tenoris counts pearls among the half's retail surprises
— the FOMC meets July 28–29 with December hike odds near 82%; the De Beers sale process moves with Penny's consortium preferred and Botswana deciding
